danger_signals.visual_form: Jewels stacking toward top of playing area
danger_signals.meaning: Game over imminent; player is losing

danger_signals.visual_form: Columns descending faster as game progresses

safety_signals.visual_form: Jewels disappearing when three or more align
safety_signals.meaning: Player action succeeded; cleared space in well

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty areas visible in playing field
safety_signals.meaning: Safe state; player has room to maneuver

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slower column fall rate; jewels descend at leisurely pace
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player has more time to position and rotate columns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Accelerated column descent speed; visual tempo increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must react faster; decision time per move decreases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rapid fall speed; well increasingly filled with jewels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pressure mounts; one error leads to game over

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Well completely filled from bottom to top with jewels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No more columns can enter; game halts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple matches disappearing in sequence as gravity resettles jewels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automatic elimination continues without player input; visual feedback of cascading success

environmental_storytelling.example: The rising well level and increasing fall speed create implicit narrative of mounting pressure without dialogue or cutscenes
Different colored jewels serve as visual language for matching logic—player learns to read and group colors as core mechanic
Multicolor jewel appearance signals occasional reprieve mechanic; its presence communicates rule variation and strategic opportunity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Descends at game-state-dependent speed; rotatable by player; stops on contact with floor or previous jewels
Disappears immediately; triggers gravity cascade and potential chain reactions
Remains static; settles under gravity after column lands
Destroys all jewels matching the color directly beneath it; acts as special power mechanic
Descends under gravity; may create new matches, triggering automatic cascade without player action

notes: Columns is a pure puzzle game with no faction, political, or narrative context. World-state communication is entirely mechanical and visual—danger and safety are expressed through gameplay physics (fall speed, well capacity) rather than thematic or narrative signals. The game's 'world' is the abstract well itself. Visual design emphasizes clarity for match-three mechanics rather than worldbuilding. Game Gear version included cosmetic variants (fruit, cards, dice) but no semantic differences in communication.
